hard.next_step: Pricing quote to be shared , while sending across some business use case .

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage S2C deal parked by the buyer, not dead: Roeland Mvokwe asked for time to consult internally on 08 May and committed to re-engage early August, so this is a buyer-set pause with a return date, not a cold deal. The initiative is unfunded until the CIO puts S2C on the roadmap, and the business case plus pricing Roeland requested have not yet been delivered to arm that internal push.
north_star_verdict.math: Created and Qualified 22 Apr 2026; close date 22 Apr 2027 is ~290 days out (a placeholder, long-runway date). One intro/discovery call 22 Apr 2026 (75 days ago). Last activity 08 May 2026 (59 days ago) is Roeland's inbound email parking the deal until early August. No buyer-facing deliverable executed since, but the buyer set the pace, so this is Slowing (stalled pending buyer's internal consult), not Off Track. Close date is not the constraint; buyer re-engagement and CIO roadmap approval are.
evidence: Single intro/discovery call 22 Apr 2026 with primary contact Roeland Mvokwe; he requested a tailored Aspen business case, pharma references, and a cost/pricing model (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026).

Buyer-set pause, not silence: Roeland emailed 08 May 2026 'kindly allow us time to consult, I will be in touch early August' (SF Task, 08 May 2026) - a concrete buyer re-engagement date, which corrects the prior 'Off Track/cold' read.

Deal is unfunded and pre-RFP: Roeland said 'I do not have this yet in our technology roadmap, and I need our CIO to take this requirement into a roadmap... so I can move forward with business case and be able to go into an RFP later on' (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026).

The prospect-requested business case and pricing (the arming material for the CIO push) have not yet been delivered; Next_Step__c still reads 'Pricing quote to be shared, while sending across some business use case'.
Decision Maker Kelly-Ann Schooling and the named approvers (CIO, Roeland's manager) have had zero engagement; the deal is single-threaded on Roeland.

sources: Avoma intro call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland Mvokwe
Zycus reference case cited by Caroline Lacocque, 22 Apr 2026
metrics.narrative: Roeland Mvokwe articulated a clear qualitative driver but never quantified it: Aspen wants one end-to-end source-to-contract platform for the group in strategic sourcing, saying 'we would love to have a one platform having all of the capabilities that we wanted from the strategic sourcing' and that their legal team's CLM tool delivered no sourcing value ('we do not see a value from the strategic sourcing point of view from that CLM tool') (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026). No Aspen-specific baseline numbers were captured (no current cycle time, headcount, spend-under-management, or savings target). The only figures on the table are Zycus proof points Caroline cited (Regeneron pharma: 70% cycle-time improvement, 200% faster supplier onboarding, 20% supplier-performance gain; 20% more spend under management, 5-7% tail savings). Closing Metrics means co-building the business case Roeland explicitly asked for using Aspen's own current-state data - his stated ask: 'work on a business case for us... give us an idea of what the cost model or the pricing model'.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged and none is named in Salesforce. Roeland himself pointed to the budget/roadmap authority: 'I need our CIO to take this requirement into a roadmap' and said he wants 'to bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO' (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026), so the funding owner is the CIO (unnamed in any Salesforce field or contact role) plus Roeland's own manager. Kelly-Ann Schooling (Head of Procurement and Supply Chain Management) holds the Salesforce Decision Maker contact role but has never been contacted. The evidence shows budget is not yet allocated at all - the S2C initiative is not on the technology roadmap - so the near-term move is to arm Roeland with the business case that gets the CIO to sponsor it, and to get names for the CIO and Roeland's manager.

sources: Avoma intro call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland Mvokwe
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria surfaced qualitatively on the intro call, driven by Roeland's operational questions rather than a formal RFP scorecard. His stated evaluation themes: a single end-to-end S2C platform (not fragmented tools), data migration ownership and control setup, SAP integration (Aspen runs finance on SAP - 'how is your experience of Zycus integrating to SAP'), implementation turnaround and post-go-live support model, and pharma reference customers ('do you have pharmaceutical industry customers'). AI/agentic capability was Zycus-positioned by Caroline, not a buyer-led criterion (consistent with AIS 5/Moderate). These are inferred-from-discovery criteria, not a confirmed weighted list; a formal criteria set would only exist once the initiative reaches the RFP Roeland flagged as a later step.

sources: Avoma intro call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland Mvokwe
decision_process.narrative: Roeland described the process he intends to run, and it is early and internal-approval-gated: (1) get the Zycus business case and pricing, (2) present internally to his manager and the CIO to get S2C onto the technology roadmap, then (3) 'move forward with business case and be able to go into an RFP later on' (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026). The 08 May email ('allow us time to consult, I will be in touch early August') shows he is in the internal-consultation step now. No formal evaluation timeline, no committee cadence, and no RFP date exist yet - the process is pre-RFP and contingent on CIO roadmap approval.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in play - the deal is at Qualified, pre-RFP, with no pricing issued. Nothing is known about MSA/SOW ownership, legal review, procurement channel, or InfoSec, and no contract-expiration forcing function was mentioned (Aspen's current Coupa/CLM arrangements were discussed only as pain, not as a dated renewal lever). Visibility here only becomes relevant after the business case wins CIO roadmap approval and an RFP begins; there is nothing to track yet, and raising paper-process items now would be premature.

sources: Avoma intro call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland Mvokwe
identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and owner-attributed. Roeland Mvokwe (Group Digital Tech Strategic Sourcing Manager) owns it: strategic sourcing has no fit-for-purpose end-to-end tool, and the CLM tool the legal team selected fails sourcing's needs - 'we do not see a value from the strategic sourcing point of view from that CLM tool' (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026). He wants to consolidate onto 'one platform having all of the capabilities that we wanted from the strategic sourcing' for the group. Inferred (not yet stated) pain: fragmentation and manual effort across Coupa plus the separate CLM tool, and reliance on SAP. The confirmed pain is real but not yet quantified - see Metrics.

sources: Avoma intro call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland Mvokwe
champion.narrative: Roeland Mvokwe is a developing, at-risk champion. What has developed: he initiated the relationship (met Caroline at an awards dinner, then set up the intro call), articulated the pain, requested a business case and pharma references to sell internally, and named the internal path ('bring Zycus to speak to both myself, manager, as well as the CIO'). What is still missing: he has made no introduction to the CIO, his manager, or Decision Maker Kelly-Ann Schooling, has not scheduled a follow-on, and on 08 May parked the evaluation to 'consult' until early August - so his advocacy is intent, not yet proven mobilisation. He is cooling relative to his fast early engagement; delivering his requested business case before August is what keeps him active.

sources: Avoma intro call 22 Apr 2026, Caroline Lacocque and Roeland Mvokwe
competition.narrative: The reconciled read: Coupa is the incumbent Aspen currently runs ('I know they're using Coupa', Caroline, 22 Apr 2026), and Aspen also has a separate legal-owned CLM tool that strategic sourcing finds valueless. Neither is a preferred rival - Roeland is actively exploring Zycus to displace/consolidate them and said 'Zycus seems to have the capacity to accommodate us... it still seems fit for us' (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026), so the buyer is not leaning to a competitor. The single strongest current threat is inertia/status-quo: the S2C project is unfunded and off the roadmap, so 'do nothing' (keep Coupa plus the separate CLM) is the likeliest outcome unless the business case forces CIO investment. Our wedge vs Coupa: native/built-in AI (Merlin intake, autonomous agents) versus Coupa's bolt-on Navi, unified single-tenant S2C with no change-order tax, and out-of-the-box SAP HANA integration.

deal_movement.summary: Reads the pulse from Next_Step, field history and the last real buyer touch: after a strong intro call 22 Apr 2026, Roeland parked the evaluation on 08 May 2026 to consult internally and return early August. The deal is stalled by the buyer's internal step, not by silence; the arming business case and pricing remain undelivered.

items.change: Intro/discovery call held; Roeland requested business case, pharma references and pricing model, and outlined a CIO-roadmap-then-RFP path
Opportunity created and moved Initial Interest -> Qualified at $200k by Caroline Lacocque

Roeland parked the evaluation: 'allow us time to consult, I will be in touch early August' - a buyer-set pause with a return date

competitive_position.summary: The strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing/status-quo inertia: Aspen's S2C project is unfunded and not yet on the CIO's technology roadmap, so continuing with Coupa plus a separate valueless CLM tool is the path of least resistance unless the business case forces investment (Roeland, 22 Apr 2026). Coupa is the incumbent Aspen runs today but the buyer is dissatisfied and actively exploring Zycus, so it is a medium not a preferred threat; the legal team's separate CLM tool is a low/dormant alternative that sourcing has already rejected. No RFP shortlist exists yet (pre-RFP).

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for an unfunded, single-threaded, buyer-paused early deal.
flags: Arm Roeland before his August consult: deliver the quantified Aspen business case with pharma references and indicative pricing so he can win CIO roadmap funding.
Break single-threading: get named intros to the CIO, Roeland's manager, and Decision Maker Kelly-Ann Schooling to de-risk internal approval.
Beat inertia, not just Coupa: make the cost of staying on Coupa plus the separate valueless CLM tool explicit, tied to Aspen's SAP-integrated end-to-end goal.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led hard on native AI (Merlin intake, autonomous negotiation/onboarding agents, control-tower governance) versus Coupa's bolt-on Navi, but the buyer engaged on operational fundamentals (SAP integration, data migration, support, pharma references), so AI is Zycus-positioned rather than buyer-demanded. Consistent with AIS 5/Moderate.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 5.0 / Moderate. The 22 Apr call confirms the read: Caroline pitched the AI-forward platform and autonomous agents heavily, but Roeland's questions centred on operational fit (data migration, SAP, support, pharma references), not AI-specific asks. Buyer is engaged but not AI-driven.

items.detail: Buyer parked the evaluation until 'early August' and close date is a 290-day placeholder; momentum depends entirely on the August re-engagement and CIO approval landing.

confidence_signals.summary: Cooling but not cold: strong intro-call engagement, then a buyer-set pause with a concrete return date (early August). One matched call, single-threaded, no follow-on booked.
items: Intro call 22 Apr 2026 was engaged and detailed (Roeland asked deep operational questions and requested a business case).
08 May 2026 inbound: 'allow us time to consult, I will be in touch early August' - a buyer-set pause, not a ghost.
No follow-on meeting booked and no second buyer contact engaged.

critical_signals.text: The 22 April intro call landed well: Roeland Mvokwe said Zycus 'seems to have the capacity to accommodate us' and asked for a tailored business case and pricing to take internally.
The business case and pricing Roeland requested have not been delivered, and the S2C project is still unfunded and off the technology roadmap.
The real threat is inertia, not a rival: Aspen can keep running dissatisfied on Coupa plus a separate valueless CLM tool unless the business case forces CIO investment.
The CIO and Roeland's manager were named as required approvers but never met, and Decision Maker Kelly-Ann Schooling has never been contacted - the deal rests entirely on Roeland.

scope_change.detail: Aspen scoped Source-to-Contract from the outset (opp created 22 Apr as 'Aspen S2C'; Roeland confirmed 'it's a source to contract, really'). No prior/broader scope to narrow from.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely interested but unfunded early-stage S2C deal that the buyer has parked until early August: Roeland Mvokwe wants Zycus but cannot move until the business case wins his CIO a roadmap slot, and no other stakeholder is engaged.

win_position.text: We are the buyer's chosen exploration and there is no preferred rival: Roeland Mvokwe said Zycus 'seems to have the capacity to accommodate us... it still seems fit for us' after the 22 Apr intro call, with incumbent Coupa disliked.
The number is held down because the initiative is unfunded and off the roadmap - Roeland must get his CIO to add S2C before any RFP, and no economic buyer is yet engaged (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026).
The deal is single-threaded on Roeland, who parked it on 08 May to consult internally until early August, so momentum is entirely dependent on that return and his internal advocacy.

deal_momentum.text: Buyer set a pause with a return date: Roeland emailed 'allow us time to consult, I will be in touch early August' on 08 May 2026, so the next real touch is weeks out.
The prospect-requested business case and pricing that would drive the next step have not yet been delivered (Next_Step__c, still open).
The buyer pulled the next step himself - Roeland requested a tailored business case, pharma references and a pricing model on the 22 Apr call rather than being pushed.

customer_commitment.text: Roeland committed to socialise Zycus internally with his manager and CIO and to progress toward an RFP (Avoma, 22 Apr 2026).
Commitment is verbal and unproven: no leadership introduction has happened, no follow-on is booked, and Aspen has not yet supplied the current-state metrics needed for the business case.

deal_risk.text: No-decision inertia is the dominant risk: Aspen can keep running Coupa plus a separate valueless CLM tool unless the business case forces CIO funding.
Power is unmapped and unengaged - the CIO (budget owner) and Decision Maker Kelly-Ann Schooling have never been contacted, leaving the deal single-threaded on a developing champion.

seniority_rising.evidence: Only Roeland (manager-level) has engaged; CIO, his manager, and the Decision Maker are absent (SF OpportunityContactRole; Avoma 22 Apr 2026).

no_decision_risk.evidence: Initiative unfunded and off the roadmap; buyer parked evaluation until early August (Roeland, 22 Apr and 08 May 2026).

customer_preference.evidence: Roeland: Zycus 'seems to have the capacity to accommodate us... it still seems fit for us' and requested a business case and pricing (22 Apr 2026) - positive lean but not decisive, and he then parked to consult internally.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain confirmed (need one end-to-end S2C platform; CLM tool gives sourcing no value) but no Aspen numbers; Zycus cited Regeneron pharma proof points, buyer baseline metrics still owed (22 Apr 2026).

items.expected_effect: Converts qualitative pain into a quantified ROI the CIO can fund.
